content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed all three fixes (journal struct drift prevention, runless-to-named completion path, plan_hash auto-population from snapshot). All 353 tests pass. The structural changes are correct: (1) journal detail structs are now defined once in model.rs with `deny_unknown_fields`, eliminating drift; `json_fill_none` provides structurally complete merge. (2) Runless-to-named completion correctly accepts runless open matches when started_at matches and backfills run_id; terminal replay path has a proper started_at guard. (3) plan_hash auto-population from snapshot is correctly applied only at entry creation time (not finalization), with explicit plan_hash taking precedence. Tests cover all three issues including happy paths, rejection cases, and precedence. One minor amendment: the `json_fill_none` approach needs a documented invariant about future conflict-worthy fields to prevent a subtle class of silent-preservation bugs.

## Amendments

1. In `json_fill_none` (fs.rs:2284-2300), the `entry().or_insert(value)` merge silently prefers base over overlay for ALL fields, including any future optional fields that may need conflict detection. Currently, conflict checks for run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail are performed explicitly before calling `json_fill_none`, but if a future optional field with conflict semantics is added to StartJournalDetails or CompletionJournalDetails, the merge will silently keep the stale base value without flagging the conflict. Add a code comment at the `json_fill_none` call sites (fs.rs:2326 and fs.rs:2367) documenting the invariant: any new optional field with conflict semantics MUST have an explicit conflict check added to `merge()` above the `json_fill_none` call, otherwise conflicting values will be silently preserved from the existing record rather than flagged as an error.
   Rationale: The json_fill_none approach correctly solves the 'silently dropped field' problem from Issue 1, but introduces a subtle 'silently preserved conflict' risk. Unlike the old per-field enumeration (which would simply omit a new field), the new approach silently keeps the existing value when both base and overlay have the same key. For fields like run_id and plan_hash this is caught by explicit checks above, but a future maintainer adding a new conflictable field might not realize they need to add a check. A comment capturing this invariant costs nothing and prevents a class of subtle bugs.
